description: Django + MySQL + TMDB Dataset movie discovery app with login, search, watchlist

tasks.title: Environment Setup
tasks.description: Install Python packages and set up MySQL database
steps: Install Python 3.11+ from python.org if not already installed
Open Command Prompt in the screenz/ project folder
Create virtual environment: python -m venv venv
Activate it: venv\Scripts\activate
Install packages: pip install -r requirements.txt
Open MySQL Workbench or MySQL CLI
Run: CREATE DATABASE screenz_db CHARACTER SET utf8mb4 COLLATE utf8mb4_unicode_ci;
Update screenz/settings.py → DATABASES → USER and PASSWORD with your MySQL credentials

tasks.title: Run Migrations
tasks.description: Create all MySQL tables from Django models
steps: Make sure virtual environment is activated (venv\Scripts\activate)
Run: python manage.py makemigrations accounts
Run: python manage.py makemigrations movies
Run: python manage.py makemigrations watchlist
Run: python manage.py migrate
Check MySQL — you should see ~15 tables in screenz_db

tasks.title: Create Superuser
tasks.description: Create an admin account to access Django Admin panel
steps: Run: python manage.py createsuperuser
Enter your email, username, and password
Visit http://127.0.0.1:8000/admin to verify it works

tasks.title: Download TMDB Dataset
tasks.description: Download the 1M TMDB movie dataset CSV from Kaggle
steps: Go to: https://www.kaggle.com/datasets/tmdb/tmdb-movie-metadata
OR: https://www.kaggle.com/datasets/rounakbanik/the-movies-dataset
Create a free Kaggle account if needed
Download the CSV file(s)
Rename/place the main movies CSV as: data/tmdb_movies.csv
The file should have columns: id, title, overview, release_date, vote_average, popularity, genres, poster_path etc.

tasks.title: Import TMDB Dataset
tasks.description: Import movies from the TMDB CSV file into MySQL database
steps: Place CSV at: data/tmdb_movies.csv
First test with 1000 movies: python scripts/import_tmdb.py data/tmdb_movies.csv 1000
If that works, run full import: python scripts/import_tmdb.py data/tmdb_movies.csv
Full 1M import can take 20-60 minutes — let it run
Check in Django Admin: http://127.0.0.1:8000/admin/movies/movie/

tasks.title: Add Platform Data
tasks.description: Seed streaming platforms (Netflix, Prime, JioCinema, Hotstar etc.)
steps: Platforms are auto-created when you run the import script (T05)
OR manually add via Django Admin: http://127.0.0.1:8000/admin/movies/platform/
Platforms: Netflix, Amazon Prime Video, JioCinema, Disney+ Hotstar, Apple TV+, ZEE5, SonyLIV
Use slugs: netflix, prime, jio, hotstar, apple, zee5, sonyliv

tasks.title: Run Dev Server & Test
tasks.description: Start Django development server and test all pages
steps: Run: python manage.py runserver
Open browser: http://127.0.0.1:8000
Test login page — sign up with a new account
Test home page — trending + genre filters
Test search — search for a movie, apply filters
Test watchlist — click + button on a movie card
Test profile — view watched movies and watchlist
Test movie detail — click any movie card

tasks.title: Collect Static Files
tasks.description: Collect all static files for production or if CSS/JS isn't loading
steps: Run: python manage.py collectstatic
If DEBUG=True in settings.py, Django serves static files automatically
Only needed for production deployment

tasks.title: Add Movie Poster Image Placeholder
tasks.description: Add a fallback image when poster is not available
steps: Create or download a placeholder image (e.g. 300x450px dark background with film icon)
Save it as: static/images/no-poster.png
The template already references this path in onerror fallback

tasks.title: MySQL Performance Indexes
tasks.description: Add database indexes for fast search with 1M movies
steps: The Movie model already has indexes defined for: release_year+vote_average, content_type+release_year, popularity
These are applied when you run migrate
For full-text search on 1M movies, optionally run these in MySQL CLI:
ALTER TABLE movies_movie ADD FULLTEXT INDEX ft_title (title);
ALTER TABLE movies_movie ADD FULLTEXT INDEX ft_overview (overview);
This enables fast MATCH AGAINST queries instead of LIKE %query%
mysql_commands: ALTER TABLE movies_movie ADD FULLTEXT INDEX ft_title (title);
ALTER TABLE movies_movie ADD FULLTEXT INDEX ft_overview (overview);

tasks.title: Build Movie Detail Page
tasks.description: Verify the detail page works with real movie data
steps: Click any movie card from home or search
URL should be: /movie/<id>/
Check poster loads (or fallback shows)
Check genres display correctly
Check watchlist button works (AJAX)
Check 'More Like This' section shows similar movies

tasks.title: Future: Add OMDB/Local Platform Mapping
tasks.description: Map movies to specific Indian streaming platforms
steps: Since TMDB API is blocked, manually assign platform availability
Option 1: Import a platform mapping CSV if available
Option 2: Use Django Admin to manually tag popular movies with platforms
Option 3: Add a 'Where to Watch' link via JustWatch scraping (advanced)
